text: We report a case of a fourteen year old male with a history of ulcerative colitis, presents with severe abdominal pain and anorexia. His past surgical history is significant for complicated three-stage pouch rectal anastomosis. Patient was diagnosed with pouch dysfunction and long rectal cuff during exam under anesthesia.

The surgeon performs transabdominal redo pouch surgery, encountering and taking down severe adhesions. The remnant mesorectum surrounding the pouch is identified, and the left superior rectal artery is dissected and taken down. Further rectal dissection is carried out for completion proctectomy, with the nonviliar's fascia visible anteriorly.

The surgeon mobilizes the ileal pouch anteriorly and laterally, using a linear stapler to dissect the ileal pouch rectal anastomosis. The pouch is separated from the remnant rectum and prepared for a new ileal pouch anal anastomosis. A 28mm circular EEA stapler anvil is secured within the pouch, and the reanastomosis is established. A leak test is performed, showing no leakage.
The surgeon creates a new loop ileostomy, and the remnant rectum is removed and sent for pathological examination.
